title: mikania paniculata
intro: Mikania paniculata, also known as Jakue, is a climbing vine native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Mikania paniculata include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Mikania paniculata can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in herbal remedies to support immune function and overall well-being.
